name: SubDDisplayParameters
summary: A collection of parameters that are passed to functions that calculate a various representations of SubD objects.

constructors.name: SubDDisplayParameters()
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance with default values.

constructors.name: SubDDisplayParameters(SerializationInfo, StreamingContext)
constructors.summary: Called by BinaryFormatter.Deserialize to create a new MeshParamaters object.

methods.name: AbsoluteDisplayDensityFromSubD
methods.signature: public static uint AbsoluteDisplayDensityFromSubD(uint adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity, SubD subd)
methods.summary: Gets absolute SubD display density for subd.

params.name: adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity
params.doc: A value <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity. When in doubt, pass SubDDisplayParameters.Density.DefaultDensity.

params.name: subd
params.doc: In the cases when the SubD in question is not available, like user interface code that applies in general and to unknown SubDs, pass SubD.Empty.

returns.doc: The absolute SubD display density is <= adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity and <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity.

methods.name: AbsoluteDisplayDensityFromSubDFaceCount
methods.signature: public static uint AbsoluteDisplayDensityFromSubDFaceCount(uint adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity, uint subDFaceCount)
methods.summary: Gets the absolute SubD display density for SubD with subdFaceCount faces.

params.name: adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity
params.doc: A value <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity. When in doubt, pass SubDDisplayParameters.Density.DefaultDensity.

returns.doc: The absolute SubD display density is <= adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity and <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity.

methods.name: ClampDisplayDensity
methods.summary: Limits display density to a value useful in user interface code.

methods.name: Coarse
methods.summary: Parameters for a course limit surface display mesh. SubD display density = adaptive SubDDisplayParameters.Density.CoarseDensity.

methods.name: CreateFromAbsoluteDisplayDensity
methods.summary: Use of absolute display density is strongly discouraged. SubDs can have a single face or millions of faces. Adaptive display meshing produces more desirable results in almost all cases.

params.name: absoluteSubDDisplayDensity
params.doc: A value <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity. When in doubt, pass SubDDisplayParameters.Density.DefaultDensity.

methods.name: CreateFromDisplayDensity
methods.summary: In most applications, the caller sets the mesh density and leaves the other parameters set to the default values.

params.name: adaptiveSubDDisplayDensity
params.doc: A value <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity. When in doubt, pass SubDDisplayParameters.Density.DefaultDensity. Values < SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MinimumAdaptiveDensity are treated as SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MinimumAdaptiveDensity. All other invalid input values are treated as SubDDisplayParameters.Density.DefaultDensity.

methods.name: CreateFromMeshDensity
methods.summary: This function creates SubDDisplayParameters from a user interface "slider" like Rhino's simple mesh controls.

returns.doc: A valid ON_SubDDisplayParameters with the specified subd display density.

methods.name: Default
methods.summary: Parameters for the default limit surface display mesh. SubD display density = adaptive SubDDisplayParameters.Density.DefaultDensity.

methods.name: DisplayDensity
methods.summary: Gets the absolute display density to use when creating display meshes for SubD. When adaptive reduction is enabled, subd.Faces.Count is used to determine the appropriate display density.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

methods.name: Empty
methods.summary: Get an empty SubDDisplayParameters.

methods.name: ExtraCoarse
methods.summary: Parameters for an extra course limit surface display mesh. SubD display density = adaptive SubDDisplayParameters.Density.ExtraCoarseDensity.

methods.name: ExtraFine
methods.summary: Parameters for an extra fine limit surface display mesh. SubD display density = adaptive SubDDisplayParameters.Density.ExtraFineDensity.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: Fine
methods.summary: Parameters for a fine limit surface display mesh. SubD display density = adaptive SubDDisplayParameters.Density.FineDensity.

methods.name: FromEncodedString
methods.summary: Converts encoded serialized string into a MeshingParameters

returns.doc: Returns a new MeshingParameters created by decoding and deserializing the string or null if value is invalid.

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

params.name: info
params.doc: The SerializationInfo to populate with data.

params.name: context
params.doc: The destination (see StreamingContext) for this serialization.

throws.when: The caller does not have the required permission.

methods.name: Medium
methods.summary: Parameters for a medium limit surface display mesh. SubD display density = adaptive SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MediumDensity.

methods.name: SetAbsoluteDisplayDensity
methods.summary: In almast all cases, you are better off using SetAdaptiveDisplayDensity().

methods.name: SetAdaptiveDisplayDensity
methods.summary: Set an adaptive SubD display density that caps display mesh quad count at SubDDisplayParameters.AdaptiveDisplayMeshQuadMaximum.

params.name: adaptiveDisplayDensity
params.doc: adaptiveDisplayDensity <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MaximumDensity. Values <= SubDDisplayParameters.Density.MinimumAdaptiveDensity will never be adaptively reduced during display mesh creation.

methods.name: ToEncodedString
methods.summary: Returns a encoded string that represents the MeshingParameters.

returns.doc: A encoded string that represents the MeshingParameters.

properties.name: AdaptiveDisplayMeshQuadMaximum
properties.summary: When the SubD display density is adaptive (default), AdaptiveMeshQuadMaximum specifies the approximate number of display mesh quads to permit before reducing the SubD display mesh density.

properties.name: DisplayDensityIsAbsolute
properties.summary: True if the SubD display density setting is absolute.

properties.name: DisplayDensityIsAdaptive
properties.summary: True if the SubD display density setting is adaptive and approximate display mesh quad count is capped at SubDDisplayParameters::AdaptiveDisplayMeshQuadMaximum.

properties.name: MeshLocation
properties.summary: The MeshLocation property determines if the mesh is on the SubD's control net or the SubD's surface.
